login.firstRun: Welcome! Create the first account — it becomes the admin.

language.heading: Interface language
language.rowDescription: Choose the language used across the GameHub interface. Your choice is saved to your account.

accessibility.reduceMotionDescription: Turn off card scaling and transition animations throughout the interface.

apiTokens.title: API Tokens
apiTokens.descSuffix: to any GameHub API endpoint. Tokens act as you.
apiTokens.copyNow: — copy it now; it won't be shown again.

oidc.enableSsoDesc: Let users sign in through your identity provider (Authentik, Keycloak, Authelia, Google…). Local username/password login keeps working alongside it.
oidc.providerConfigDesc: Issuer, client ID/secret and button label.
oidc.redirectUriLabel: Register this redirect URI with your provider:

invites.heading: Registration & Invites
invites.openRegistrationDesc: Anyone who can reach this server may create a viewer account. Turn off to require an invite link.
invites.createInviteDesc: Single-use link with a pre-assigned role, expires after 7 days.

panel.yourStatusDesc: How you appear to other people

friends.title: Friends
friends.friendRequestCount: {count, plural, one {# friend request} other {# friend requests}}
friends.noFriends: No friends yet — search above and send a request.
friends.removeConfirmBody: They'll be removed from your friends. You can send a new request anytime.

profile.nothingPlayed: Nothing played yet — launch a game and it shows up here.

raLink.title: RetroAchievements
raLink.linkDescription: Link your <link>RetroAchievements</link> account to show your achievements and unlock progress on game pages. Paste your Web API key — it's read-only and stored encrypted.
raLink.credentialsNote: Your achievement lists and unlock progress are pulled with your own RA credentials and shown on each game's page and your profile.
raLink.findKeyHint: Find your key at retroachievements.org → Settings → Keys (the “Web API Key”).

log.confirmClearAll: Delete the ENTIRE activity log? This can't be undone.
log.confirmClearScope: Delete activity log entries {scope}? This can't be undone.
log.exportTitle: Download the whole log as a JSON file
log.emptyState: No activity recorded yet. Run a scan or change a setting and it'll show up here.

activityLog.title: Activity Log
activityLog.subtitle: Live system events — scans, scrapes, users, settings and maintenance.

apiDocs.title: API Documentation
apiDocs.authorizeIntro: Authorize with a personal API token from your
apiDocs.authorizeOutro: (click Authorize → bearerAuth), or just use your current browser session.

appearance.heading: Appearance
appearance.introSuffix: (the Steam Deck CSS Loader library). GameHub speaks the same class vocabulary as SteamOS, so most visual themes apply — coverage varies by theme. Themes affect everyone; changes apply on next page load.
appearance.uninstallConfirmBody: Its auto-installed dependencies are removed too, unless another installed theme still needs them.

jobIndicator.jobsScheduled: {count, plural, one {# job scheduled} other {# jobs scheduled}}

shelves.emptyHint: Open Filter, choose what you want, then “Save as Dynamic Collection” — or add games to a collection from a game’s options.

newForm.smartRowDescription: Membership comes from filters and stays in sync automatically.
newForm.smartHint: Membership comes from filters and stays in sync as you scan and edit metadata. Fields combine with AND; inclusion only.

deleteButton.confirmBody: The games themselves are not removed from your library.

card.smartTitle: Smart collection — membership comes from filters and stays in sync

index.title: Collections
index.empty: No collections yet. Create one above, or add games from their detail pages.
index.smartTitle: Smart collection — membership comes from filters and stays in sync automatically
index.autoGeneratedNote: Auto-generated from metadata — read-only and always up to date as you scrape. Groups with fewer than 5 games are hidden.
index.emptyVirtual: Nothing yet — scrape some metadata and genres, developers, and publishers will group themselves here.

layout.noController: No controller detected — press a button on it to identify it.
layout.inheritsNote: This scope currently inherits its layout.
layout.scopeNoteGlobal: Default layout for this controller type. Per-system and per-game layouts override it.
layout.scopeNoteSystem: Applies to every game on this system, overriding the global default. Per-game layouts still win.
layout.scopeNoteGame: Applies to this game only, overriding the system and global layouts.

page.nothingRunning: Nothing is running right now — scans and scrapes you start appear here, and anything waiting shows under Scheduled.

emulator.loadEmulatorError: Couldn't load the emulator. Check your connection and try again.
emulator.loadTimeoutError: This is taking longer than expected. The game or core may be unavailable.

folderPicker.containerNote: Running in a container: SMB/NAS shares must be mounted as volumes (they appear here as normal folders, e.g. /roms).

firmware.title: BIOS — {name}
firmware.description: Only files named exactly as the emulator expects are imported — extras are ignored. A correctly-named file whose hash doesn't match is kept but marked unverified. Drop a .zip to import a whole set. Loaded by the emulator automatically.
firmware.noBios: {name} doesn't use a BIOS — nothing to upload.
firmware.unverifiedTitle: Correct filename, but the content hash doesn't match the known BIOS — may still work
firmware.outcomeRejected: ✗ {filename}: name not an expected BIOS — skipped

romUpload.title: Upload ROMs — {name}
romUpload.description: Files go into this system's mapped library folder and are scanned in immediately. Existing files are never overwritten. 512MB per file max — for big disc images, copy them to the share directly and rescan.
romUpload.added: {count, plural, one {✓ Added # file and scanned them in.} other {✓ Added # files and scanned them in.}}

saveStates.batteryUploaded: ✓ Battery save uploaded — it loads next time you play
saveStates.batteryMeta: {size} · updated {date} — loads automatically when you play
saveStates.batteryNoneYet: None yet — it syncs automatically while you play (and on exit).
saveStates.noSaveStatesHint: While playing, open the player's ⚙ menu and press Save State — it syncs here automatically.
saveStates.deleteBatteryBody: Your next play session starts fresh.

gallery.removesFromGame: This removes it from the game.

video.title: video snap
video.hint: Now playing in the game's Media section

gameOptions.noGamesFound: No games found — try a shorter name.
gameOptions.nothingForMatch: Nothing came back for that match
gameOptions.enterCollectionName: Enter a name for the collection
gameOptions.createCollectionDesc: A simple way to organize your library — add this game now and more whenever you like.
gameOptions.dynamicCollectionDesc: Dynamic collections use filters to build a list that updates as your library grows.
gameOptions.matchHelp: Pick the correct game — its metadata and artwork replace whatever the automatic file match found.
gameOptions.hideTooltip: Hidden games disappear from your grids and shelves — find them under the Hidden tab in Library
gameOptions.backfillTooltip: Fill only missing text fields + the IGDB trailer and related games — never overwrites existing data and downloads no artwork
gameOptions.fixMatchTooltip: Search the game databases by name and pick the right match when the automatic one is wrong or missing
gameOptions.fetchVideoTooltip: Fetches this game's video snap even when videos are disabled in the global scraping settings
gameOptions.fetchManualTooltip: Fetches this game's PDF manual even when manuals are disabled in the global scraping settings
gameOptions.patchTooltip: Apply an IPS/UPS/BPS patch in the browser and download the result

gamePage.noDescription: No description yet — scrape metadata to fill this in.
gamePage.saveStatesInBrowser: {count, plural, one {# save state in your browser} other {# save states in your browser}}
gamePage.readyToPlay: Ready to play in your browser
gamePage.allDiscsZipTitle: Download every disc as one zip

notes.placeholder: Notes to self — passwords, where you left off, that one secret exit…

variants.otherVersions: Other versions of this game on {system} — regional releases, hacks and translations.

romPatcher.patched: ✓ Patched — downloading "{name}". Drop it into the ROM folder and rescan to add it to the library.
romPatcher.intro: Apply a translation or hack patch (.ips, .ups, .bps) right in the browser. Your original file is never touched — the patched copy downloads to your device.

home.noActivity: No activity from other players yet.
home.nothingToRecommend: Nothing to recommend yet — add and play more games to build your picks.

library.appsHiddenDueToFilter: {count, plural, one {# app} other {# apps}} hidden due to filter
library.noFavorites: No favorites yet — star games to add them here.
library.subtitleSystem: Choose which games from this system to show. Filters stay until you clear them.
library.subtitleCollection: Choose which games from this collection to show. Filters stay until you clear them.
library.subtitleLibrary: Choose which games to show when browsing your Library. Filters apply to every collection and stay until you clear them.
library.saveModalDesc: Membership comes from these filters and stays in sync automatically as your library changes.

maintenancePanel.title: Maintenance
maintenancePanel.intro: Automatic scanning and cleanup of games whose files were moved or deleted.
maintenancePanel.dailyScanDesc: Automatically re-scan all folders once a day.
maintenancePanel.autoCleanupDesc: When a scan marks games missing, remove them (and their media) right away — manual, per-system, and daily scans alike. ROM files are never touched.
maintenancePanel.watchFoldersDesc: Rescan automatically when files are added or removed (takes effect after a server restart; unreliable on network shares).
maintenancePanel.computeHashesDesc: CRC32/MD5/SHA1 per ROM — enables exact game identification via Hasheous. Runs in the background; files over 256MB are skipped.
maintenancePanel.rescrapeArtDesc: Re-fetch the hero, logo, icon and ribbon art for every system in your library, in your configured provider order. Use after changing providers or the art mapping. Runs in the background.
maintenancePanel.refreshImagesDesc: Rebuild the browse-card and hero collage images for systems whose covers changed. Runs automatically after scans and scrapes; use this to force it.
maintenancePanel.refreshStarted: ✓ Refreshing changed collage images in the background.
maintenancePanel.cleanupCounts: {missing, plural, one {# missing game} other {# missing games}}, {orphan, plural, one {# orphaned media folder} other {# orphaned media folders}}
maintenancePanel.confirmBody: Removes {missing, plural, one {# missing game} other {# missing games}} and {orphan, plural, one {# orphaned media folder} other {# orphaned media folders}}. Favorites and collection entries for those games are removed too. ROM files are never touched.

media.label: Scraped media
media.hint: Box art, videos, manuals — can be many GB; re-scrapable.

backupPanel.createBackupDesc: One .tar archive of the database (always included) plus the items above. ROM files and the credential-encryption key are never included — back those up separately.
backupPanel.warningBody: Your metadata-provider and single sign-on credentials are encrypted at rest with a secret key that is deliberately not included in backups — so a stolen backup can't decrypt them.
backupPanel.envKeyDesc: This install uses the {secretKeyEnv} environment variable. Store that value in a safe place (a password/secrets manager). Set the same value on any host you restore to and your credentials will decrypt automatically.
backupPanel.fileKeyDesc: The key lives at {keyPath}. If you lose it — or restore a backup onto a fresh machine without it — those credentials cannot be recovered and must be re-entered. Download it now and store it somewhere safe (or set {secretKeyEnv} to a value you control).
backupPanel.restoreDesc: Replaces the database and any folders contained in the backup file. Everyone is signed out afterwards.
backupPanel.restoreConfirmBody: The current database is replaced — users, playtime, settings, everything — along with any folders the backup contains. Everyone is signed out and you'll sign back in with an account from the backup.
backupPanel.restoreConfirmNote: The previous database is kept as {backupName}. ROM files are not affected.
backupPanel.restoredSuccess: Restored {items} ({files} files, backup from {date}). Redirecting to sign-in…

reportsPanel.datNotLoaded: The DAT hash database isn't loaded — load it under Settings → Metadata Sources to verify hashes against known dumps.
reportsPanel.badHashesTitle: Bad hashes (don't match the DAT for a known dump)
reportsPanel.hashSummary: {verified} verified · {mismatch} bad · {unknown} not in DAT (across {systems} systems).
reportsPanel.pickReport: Pick a report to generate. Each runs on its own — the slower ones are marked.

account.statusDescription: How you appear to other people

apiTokens.tokensIntro1: For scripts and integrations — send a
apiTokens.tokensIntro3: header of any GameHub API request. Tokens act as you.
apiTokens.copyNow: — copy it now; it won't be shown again.

retroAchievements.raIntro2: account to show your achievements and unlock progress on game pages. Paste your read-only Web API key — it's stored encrypted.
retroAchievements.raLinkedNote: Your achievement lists and unlock progress are pulled with your own RA credentials and shown on each game's page and your profile.
retroAchievements.findKeyHint: Find it at retroachievements.org → Settings → Keys.

mobileDownloads.quotaPaused: Paused — a provider hit its request limit. Resumes when the window resets.
mobileDownloads.nothingRunning: Nothing is running right now — scans and scrapes appear here, and anything waiting shows under Scheduled.

mobileHome.nothingNew: Nothing new to report just yet.
mobileHome.noActivity: No recent activity yet — play something to kick things off.
mobileHome.noRecommended: Play and scrape a few games and personalized picks will show up here.

profileEdit.aboutDesc: Set your profile name and details. This is how other users on this GameHub see you.
profileEdit.profileUrlNote: Your profile is available at: /profile/{id} (signed-in users only)
profileEdit.avatarHint: Upload a file from your device. Image should be square, at least 184px × 184px.
profileEdit.backgroundDesc: Choose a background to show on your profile page — pulled from your favorite and most-played games' hero art, or upload your own.
profileEdit.noMatchingHeroArt: No games matching “{query}” have hero art.
profileEdit.noHeroArt: No hero art yet — scrape some games (or favorite a few) and their heroes appear here.
profileEdit.themeDesc: Choose a color theme for your profile page.
profileEdit.badgeDesc: Choose a badge to feature at the top of your profile.

content.attribution: Related content from IGDB & your picks

manager.noneYet: None yet — search above to link another game in your library.

hero.label: Hero banner
hero.hint: Wide art for the game page header

theme.label: Theme music
theme.hint: MP3/OGG title theme — plays on the game page (beats the YouTube match)

manual.label: Manual
manual.hint: PDF game manual — opens from the game page

romProperties.systemDesc: Changes which emulator core is used.
romProperties.boxartUrlDesc: Leave empty to use a generated cover.
romProperties.matchFilenameDesc: Look up libretro-thumbnails using the ROM's filename.
romProperties.matchTitleDesc: Look up libretro-thumbnails using the title field.
romProperties.heroUrlDesc: Wide art behind the game page header. Scrapers fill this automatically.
romProperties.iconUrlDesc: Square game icon (stored for future use).
romProperties.mediaIntro: Upload your own files to replace anything the scrapers fetched. Files are stored in GameHub's media folder — ROM files are never touched.
romProperties.themeDescFile: An uploaded theme file is set — it plays instead of YouTube.
romProperties.themeDescYt: This video plays on the game page. Paste a different URL to change it.
romProperties.themeDescNone: No match yet — the first YouTube result is found automatically when the game page is opened, or pin one here.
romProperties.scrapeDesc: Fetch description, art, and video from your configured providers.
romProperties.descriptionPlaceholder: No description — scrape or write one.
romProperties.renameDesc: Renames the actual file on disk (the extension must stay the same).
romProperties.dangerIntro: Removes the game from the library along with its downloaded media, favorites, and save states.
romProperties.dangerDeleteFile: The ROM file itself will be PERMANENTLY deleted from disk.
romProperties.dangerKeepFile: The ROM file stays on disk, so the game reappears on the next scan.
romProperties.alsoDeleteFile: Also permanently delete the file from disk
romProperties.deleteConfirmBodyDelete: This permanently deletes {filename} from disk, along with its library entry, downloaded media, favorites and save states. This cannot be undone.
romProperties.deleteConfirmBodyKeep: Removes {filename} from the library (media, favorites, save states). The ROM file stays on disk, so it reappears on the next scan.

common.pickSystems: Pick the systems to include, or scrape all.

bulk.intro: Fetches art and details in the background — navigate away, keep playing, or close the browser and it carries on. Come back here to watch or cancel.
bulk.runningNotice: A scrape is running — track its progress on the <link>downloads page</link>. Starting another below just queues it.
bulk.scrapeMissingDesc: Fetch art and details only for games that have none yet.
bulk.scrapeEverythingDesc: Re-fetch art and details for every game, replacing existing metadata.
bulk.backfillDesc: Fill only empty fields; never overwrites, and downloads no artwork.
bulk.queued: Queued — starts after the current job finishes. Track it on the downloads page.
bulk.missingModalDesc: Only games that have never been scraped will be fetched.
bulk.backfillModalDesc: Only empty metadata fields are filled in (text, trailers and related games) — existing metadata and artwork are left untouched, and no art is downloaded.
bulk.everythingModalDesc: Every game will be re-imported from scratch: <hl>existing downloaded art and metadata are cleared first</hl>, then re-fetched.
bulk.noConfiguredSystems: No configured systems to scrape — add folders under Library first.

systemMeta.metaBlurb: Fetches each console's manufacturer, type, year and media format from ScreenScraper.
systemMeta.metaDone: {count, plural, one {Updated info for # system.} other {Updated info for # systems.}}
systemMeta.artBlurb: Force-fetches each console's hero, logo, icon and ribbon from your configured providers, replacing existing art.
systemMeta.artDone: {count, plural, one {Updated artwork for # system.} other {Updated artwork for # systems.}}
systemMeta.noSystems: No systems in your library yet.

scraperOptions.title: Scraper options
scraperOptions.intro: Choose which provider wins when both have the same item, and which items to fetch. Changes save automatically and apply to single-game and library scrapes.
scraperOptions.preferredSourceHint: Tried first for this item; the priority order fills in if it has none.

hashMatching.label: Hash matching (Hasheous)
hashMatching.description: When file hashes are computed (Settings → Library → Compute hashes), identify games by exact hash before scraping. Needs IGDB configured.

boxStyle.label: Box art style
boxStyle.description: ScreenScraper offers flat 2D scans or 3D box renders.

concurrency.label: Parallel downloads
concurrency.description: How many games a library scrape processes at once. Automatically capped to the number of threads your ScreenScraper account allows, so it never trips the API — a higher setting just means “use all my threads.”

providerNotes.launchbox: metadata + art from a local database — import it under Providers
providerNotes.libretro: box art + screenshots — free, no account needed

details.label: Game details
details.desc: Developer, publisher, genre, players, rating, release date, age rating, language, region, franchise, game modes, themes.

boxart.label: Box art
boxart.desc: The cover shown in your library.

hero.label: Hero banners
hero.desc: Wide art behind the game page header and home hero tile.

icon.label: Icons
icon.desc: Square game icons (stored for future use).

screenshot.label: Screenshots
screenshot.desc: In-game shots shown on game pages.

badges.label: Studio & rating badges
badges.desc: Developer, publisher and age-rating logo images.

setIntegrity.title: Set Integrity
setIntegrity.datNotConfigured: Import the hash database first (Metadata Sources › No-Intro / Redump / MAME). Once imported, GameHub can verify every ROM against known-good dumps, find duplicates, and report which games you're missing.
setIntegrity.introLead: Verifies your hashed ROMs against the local DAT database and reports problems.
setIntegrity.introVerifiedDesc: = the file matches a known-good dump exactly.
setIntegrity.introBadHackDesc: = a known title whose hash doesn't match any dump (a bad dump, romhack, or odd revision).
setIntegrity.introUnknownDesc: = not in any loaded DAT. Auditing never modifies or deletes ROM files.
setIntegrity.auditDesc: Classifies every hashed ROM. {count, plural, one {# system} other {# systems}} have DAT coverage.
setIntegrity.rehashComplete: Re-hash complete — run the audit to refresh verdicts.
setIntegrity.rehashLead: Recompute hashes for .zip/.7z ROMs from their
setIntegrity.rehashTail: file so they match No-Intro/Redump. Run once if your archives were hashed before this update, then re-run the audit.
setIntegrity.duplicatesDesc: Byte-identical ROMs (same MD5), across every folder and system.
setIntegrity.duplicateGroups: {count, plural, one {# duplicate group} other {# duplicate groups}} · {bytes} redundant
setIntegrity.titleDupDesc: One game held as several region/revision copies. Keep your favorite; hide the rest — they move to the Hidden tab and are never deleted.
setIntegrity.titleDupCount: {count, plural, one {# game} other {# games}} with extra copies · {redundant} redundant
setIntegrity.hideExtras: Hide {count, plural, one {# extra} other {# extras}}
setIntegrity.hidExtras: Hid {count, plural, one {# redundant copy} other {# redundant copies}} — they're in the Hidden tab and can be restored anytime.
setIntegrity.missingFromSetDesc: How complete each system is versus its full DAT set (distinct titles, main library only).
setIntegrity.regionNaDesc: Falls back where there's no NA release
setIntegrity.regionEuDesc: Falls back where there's no EU release
setIntegrity.regionJpDesc: Falls back where there's no JP release

audio.uiSoundsDesc: Play navigation and confirmation sounds throughout the interface.
audio.gameThemeMusicDesc: Play a game's theme when you open its page, if one is set.
audio.soundPackDesc: Replaces the UI sound effects. Applies to everyone on next page load.
audio.musicPackDesc: Loops menu music while you browse; pauses in-game.

automation.intro: These processes run in the background on a schedule. Toggle them on/off, set how often they run, and trigger any of them right now.
automation.autoScanDesc: Rescans your folders on a schedule · last {when}
automation.autoCleanDesc: Remove games whose files disappeared, after a scan
automation.watchFoldersDesc: Rescan automatically when library files are added/removed
automation.newsRefreshDesc: How often the home News feeds are re-fetched
automation.newsRefreshNoFeeds: No external feeds configured — add them under Settings › News
automation.backupDescDefault: Write a .tar backup of the database, saves & firmware on a schedule

news.homePageNewsDesc: Controls the What's New tab on the home page. GameHub updates and library milestones appear automatically.
news.romHackingNewsDesc: Pulls recent posts from the feeds below into the What's New tab, refreshed automatically every few hours.

settingsController.noControllerDesc: Connect a controller (USB or Bluetooth) and press any button — browsers only reveal a gamepad after its first input.
settingsController.moveFocusDesc: D-Pad and the left stick move focus and can't be remapped — they're fixed to navigation.
settingsController.emulatorLayoutDesc: Map your controller's buttons to each console's buttons — a global default per controller type, with per-system and per-game overrides. Also reachable from a system's tools and a game's play bar.
settingsController.movementDesc: D-Pad and both sticks are fixed to movement in game, just like navigation.
settingsController.rewindDesc: Buffer recent gameplay so you can rewind in-game (via the emulator's Rewind control). Uses extra memory and CPU — leave off on low-powered devices. Applies to games you start after changing it, on this device.
settingsController.testIntro: Press buttons and move the D-Pad/stick — each input lights up on the controller so you can identify it.
settingsController.pressButtonPrompt: Press the button for this action…

datDb.pickAtLeastOne: Pick at least one DAT set to import.
datDb.importedDats: Imported {count, plural, one {# DAT} other {# DATs}} ({games} games)
datDb.datIntro: The DAT database is a free catalog of verified game dumps keyed by their file hashes (CRC32, MD5, SHA1) — sourced from No-Intro (cartridges), Redump (discs) and MAME (arcade) via the libretro-database project. GameHub downloads the DAT files once and imports them into a local database, so it can match your ROMs to their exact canonical names by hash — which fixes renamed or oddly-titled dumps and makes downloadable art (named to these same sets) line up 1:1. Fully offline afterward; re-import any time to pull in newer sets.
datDb.categoriesNote: Cartridges + discs cover almost every console. Arcade and computer sets are large downloads — enable only if you have those systems.
datDb.importedStatus: Imported {games} games and {entries} hash entries across {systems} systems
datDb.notImported: Not yet imported — download the DAT files to enable hash matching.
datDb.customDatIntro: Upload your own clrmamepro .dat files (e.g. straight from No-Intro Dat-o-Matic or Redump) to extend hash matching to sets GameHub doesn't fetch automatically. Custom DATs are kept separate and survive re-imports of the sets above. If the DAT's system name matches one of your platforms it maps automatically; otherwise it still adds hashes for canonical naming.
datDb.datFormatNote: clrmamepro .dat format · up to 300 MB each

firmware.uploadingFiles: Uploading {count, plural, one {# file} other {# files}}…
firmware.notExpectedBios: name not an expected BIOS — skipped
firmware.uploadIndividualDesc: Pick a system, then choose one or more BIOS files. Each is matched by content hash and filed under the exact name the emulator needs — even if it was renamed.
firmware.importZipDesc: Drop in a zip of BIOS files and every one is auto-matched to its system by content hash. Leave the scope on “All systems”, or limit it to a single console.
firmware.noBiosYet: No BIOS uploaded yet. Turn on “Show optional” to see everything.

internet.givesScreenscraper: Descriptions, genres, players, ratings, dates, 2D/3D box art, screenshots, videos.
internet.givesIgdb: Modern curated metadata, franchises, covers, screenshots.
internet.givesMobygames: Deep long-tail coverage of obscure and older titles.
internet.givesThegamesdb: Community metadata plus boxart, fanart, clear logos and screenshots.
internet.givesSteamgriddb: Steam-style hero banners, grids and logos.
internet.givesEmumovies: Video snaps and scanned game manuals (supporter account, via FTP).
internet.ignoreTlsNote: Only enable if the connection test fails with a certificate error (e.g. <c>CERT_HAS_EXPIRED</c>) while screenscraper.fr is otherwise up. Applies only to <c>api.screenscraper.fr</c> and disables certificate verification for it, which weakens protection against interception. Turn it back off once ScreenScraper fixes their cert.

settingsStorage.romLibraryFolderDesc: The folder that contains your systems.
settingsStorage.detectSystemsDesc: Scan the folder above and map each subfolder to a system.
settingsStorage.scanAllDesc: Find new files and mark removed ones missing across every configured system.
settingsStorage.checkForNewSystemsDesc: Register any newly-supported consoles that aren't in your library yet.
settingsStorage.groupSummary: {sysCount, plural, one {# system} other {# systems}} · {games} games
settingsStorage.noSystemsConfigured: No systems configured yet — point auto-detect at your ROM root above.
settingsStorage.editFoldersDesc: Add or change this system's ROM folders and variants
settingsStorage.rescanDesc: Find new files and mark removed ones missing
settingsStorage.scrapeMissingDesc: Fetch art & details only for games that have none yet
settingsStorage.scrapeAllDesc: Re-fetch art & details for every game in this system
settingsStorage.noFoldersForSystem: No folders for {name} yet — add its main folder, plus extra rows for variant libraries (hacks, translations, digital…).
settingsStorage.variantTitle: Empty = main library. e.g. hacks, translations, digital
settingsStorage.scanStarted: Scan started — see the header or downloads page
settingsStorage.allSystemsUpToDate: All systems are up to date — no new systems found.
settingsStorage.addedNewSystems: Added {count, plural, one {# new system} other {# new systems}}: {names}.

keyboard.textEntryDesc: When a controller is in use, an on-screen keyboard docks automatically as soon as a text field is focused — no device keyboard needed. Navigate the keys with the D-pad, press A to type, and B to close it. A connected keyboard still works for typing at any time.

launchBox.aboutBefore: The LaunchBox Games Database is a free, community-maintained catalog of over 130,000 games across virtually every console and computer platform, with box art, clear logos, fan art, screenshots, developers, publishers, genres, release dates, ratings and descriptions. GameHub downloads the full
launchBox.aboutAfter: once (~500 MB) and imports it into a local database, so scraping from it afterward is completely offline — no API keys, no accounts, no rate limits, and much faster than the online providers. It's the best first source to enable: re-import any time to pull in the latest community additions.
launchBox.importedStatus: Imported {games, plural, one {# game} other {# games}}, {images, plural, one {# image} other {# images}} and {systems, plural, one {# system} other {# systems}}
launchBox.notImported: Not yet imported — download the database to enable offline scraping.
launchBox.importing: Importing — {games, plural, one {# game} other {# games}}, {images, plural, one {# image} other {# images}}, {systems, plural, one {# system} other {# systems}}…

system.updateChannelDesc: Which image tag this install follows.
system.clock24Desc: Show the top-bar clock in 24-hour format.
system.systemReportDesc: Save a diagnostic snapshot of this install as JSON.
system.legalNoticesDesc: Open-source software, emulation, data providers and datasets GameHub relies on.

users.heading: Users
users.description: Everyone shares the same game library; favorites, playtime, collections and save states are per user. Viewers browse & play, Editors also edit metadata, Admins manage everything.
users.addSomeone: Add someone to your household with a role.
users.signsOut: Signs them out everywhere so the new password takes effect.
users.noProfilesCreateIn: No profiles yet — create one in
users.assignProfile: Assign a profile to limit what this account can see and play.

age.heading: Age Restrictions
age.description: Create restriction profiles — a set of allowed systems and a maximum content rating — then assign one to any account under Users. A restricted account only sees and can open the games its profile permits, everywhere in the app.
age.createOneHint: Create one (e.g. “Little kids”, “Teens”) to start restricting accounts.
age.name: Name
age.noSystems: No systems in the library yet.
age.hideUnratedLabel: Also hide unrated games (strict — most games have no rating yet)
age.deleteWarning: This removes the profile from {count, plural, one {# assigned user} other {# assigned users}} — they'll go back to full, unrestricted access.
age.noUsersAssigned: No users are assigned this profile.

status.detectNoSystems: No system folders in there yet — that's fine, save it and add games whenever you're ready.
status.detectNothingRecognized: Nothing recognized as a system folder — you can still save this folder and map systems later in Settings → Library.
status.importing: Importing — {count, plural, one {# game} other {# games}}…
status.gamesImported: {count, plural, one {# game imported} other {# games imported}}

welcome.intro: Your self-hosted retro game library with a SteamOS-style interface. In the next couple of minutes we'll point it at your ROM folders, hook up metadata sources, and run the first scan. Everything here can be changed later in Settings.
welcome.skipSetup: Skip setup — I'll configure everything myself
welcome.restorePrompt: Moving from another install? Restore from a backup
welcome.restoreUploadPost: made in Settings → Backup & restore. It brings back the database (accounts, library, playtime, settings) and whatever was included (saves, firmware, media). This replaces the account you just created — you'll sign in with an account from the backup afterwards.
welcome.restoreSuccess: Restored {items} — {files} files from a v{version} backup.
welcome.signInRestored: Sign in with your restored account

library.heading: Where are your ROMs?
library.descPre: Point at the folder that contains one subfolder per system (e.g.
library.descPost: work too). An empty folder is fine if you're starting from scratch — scans never modify your files; ROMs are only touched when you explicitly rename, delete, or upload them with the admin tools.
library.recognizedSystems: Recognized {count, plural, one {# system folder} other {# system folders}}
library.unmatchedFolders: {count, plural, one {# unrecognized folder} other {# unrecognized folders}}
library.mapLater: map them later in Settings → Library.

metadata.heading: Metadata & artwork
metadata.introRest: GameHub works out of the box with the free sources at the bottom — each account you add just means better matches and richer game pages. All of this can be set up later in Settings → Metadata Providers.
metadata.lbGives: Titles, descriptions, genres, release dates, ratings and box art for 183k+ games.
metadata.lbHowto: Nothing to sign up for — one ~100 MB download and everything is local from then on. The best first pick.
metadata.ssGives: The deepest retro database — descriptions, genres, player counts, ratings, release dates, 2D/3D box art, screenshots and video snaps.
metadata.ssHowtoPost: and sign in with your account — GameHub ships with its own app credentials, so that's all you need.
metadata.igdbGives: Modern, well-curated metadata — descriptions, genres, franchises, ratings, covers and screenshots.
metadata.igdbHowtoPre: Sign in with a (free) Twitch account at
metadata.igdbHowtoPost: , register an application, and copy its Client ID and Client Secret.
metadata.mobyGives: Huge long-tail coverage — descriptions, genres, covers and screenshots for obscure and older titles other sources miss.
metadata.mobyHowtoPre: Request a free non-commercial API key at
metadata.sgdbGives: Steam-style hero banners, grids and logos — the big header art on game pages.
metadata.sgdbHowtoPost: and generate a key under Profile → Preferences → API.
metadata.emGives: Video snaps and scanned game manuals (plus alternate artwork), downloaded over FTP.
metadata.emHowtoPost: account — FTP downloads need a supporter subscription.
metadata.alwaysOnDesc4: (Flash game metadata) — these work automatically with no keys or accounts.

firstScan.desc: Reads every mapped folder and builds the library. Big collections over the network can take a few minutes.
firstScan.gamesAdded: {count, plural, one {# game added} other {# games added}}
firstScan.filesScanned: {count, plural, one {# file scanned} other {# files scanned}}
firstScan.startScraping: Start scraping metadata in the background
firstScan.scrapingBackground: Scraping in the background — watch it in Settings → Scraping

done.heading: You're all set
done.descPre: A few things worth knowing: the home screen fills up as metadata arrives · press
done.descPost: or the controller's Start button for the main menu · games play right in your browser (no installs) · BIOS files go in Settings → Firmware · invite your household from Settings → Users.

systemTools.scanStarted: Scan started — see the header or downloads page
systemTools.scrapeQueued: Queued behind the current job — track it on the downloads page.
systemTools.scrapeStarted: Scraping {name} started — see the header or downloads page.
systemTools.nothingToClean: Nothing to clean — no {name} games are marked missing.
systemTools.cleanupRemoved: Removed {games, plural, one {# missing game} other {# missing games}} and {folders, plural, one {# media folder} other {# media folders}}.
systemTools.noArtFoundConfigure: No {kind} found — configure SteamGridDB/ScreenScraper for choices
systemTools.noInfoFound: No system info found for this console.
systemTools.neverShow: Never show a {kind} for this system
systemTools.backfillTitle: Fill only missing text fields + IGDB trailers and related games across this system — never overwrites existing data and downloads no artwork
systemTools.controllerLayoutTitleAttr: Controller button layout for all {name} games
systemTools.gamelistTitle: EmulationStation / ES-DE metadata (drop in the ROM folder)
systemTools.retroarchTitle: RetroArch playlist with real paths + CRC
systemTools.cleanupConfirm: Remove {count} missing {name} {count, plural, one {game} other {games}} and their media? This cannot be undone.
